## Visa Fees
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of USD 50 per application. As of the 03 March 2019, the Service Fee has to be paid online when scheduling an appointment
                                                                                to submit the application; without pre-payment of the service fee, you will not be able to secure an appointment. For more information about the pre-payment of the service

[Table:  Relatives Visa]

## Documents Required

Please Click here to view the Checklist.
Notes:
- All documents in a foreign language must be translated into English by a sworn translator.
- Please submit only certified copies of original documents except Police Clearance(s). The Mission will not be held responsible if the original document is lost.
- Submission of Fake/Fraudulent documents is an offense. The Mission may refer such cases to relevant law enforcement authorities.
- The Mission may request any additional documents / information where and when necessary.
- Consent letter from parent(s) for child/children travelling with one parent or alone needs to be notarized.
- Official passports will be submitted at South African High Commission

## Photo Specifications
 2 photographs must be provided meeting the below specifications
The photo must: 
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35 by 45mm (width x height)
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses: 
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible. 
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress: 
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown. 
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
                                                                                    A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Normal case processing times
The normal processing time is 8 weeks.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
- A financial undertaking or support by a foreigner (parents/ spouse of an applicant who is not a South African citizen) is no longer recognized in terms of the South African Immigration
                                                                                Regulations.
-  For ALL applicants 18 years and over applying under a visitor’s visa, volunteer visa, study visa and accompanying dependents:
- The bank statements must be provided in the applicant’s name and must be sufficient to cover the full duration of their visit / volunteer period/ studies in South Africa.
- If the amount is being transferred to the applicant’s account from the parent’s / spouse account, a letter must be written by the applicant stating that the funds transferred
                                                                                        will be used for the sole purpose of their visit in South Africa.
-  Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete (For
                                                                                    an application to be considered complete, all couples and families applying together must still provide complete sets of required documents for EACH applicant. This includes
                                                                                    copies of bank statements, flight tickets, accommodation, marriage certificate certified copies (where applicable), medical reports (where applicable) and radiological
                                                                                    reports (where applicable) for minors and dependants) :
- All copies of ID/Passport/Residence permit of host in South Africa must be certified copies – that is, certified by the Police in South Africa or a Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- All bank statements must be stamped by the bank, or accompanied by a separate letter from the bank verifying the account, name and address of the client.
- For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and an affidavit signed by both biological parents
                                                                                        and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths will be required. Additional documents will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable to sign.
- All applicants must apply in person.
- No fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- We strongly recommend that you pay careful attention to the Overview, Documents Required and Processing Time sections of your chosen category, before proceeding with your application.
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that
                                                                                the embassy will grant your request.
- Please note applicants should be aware of the processing time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as
                                                                                stated, and in some cases may take longer
- When granted, the authorisation should be for specified limited time-frame, to work for a specific entity and at a specific place, for instance, economic, medical, academic, sporting,
                                                                                cultural or social (e.g. religious) application.
- The specific entity referred to in paragraph (c) above must identify itself and be a bona fide corporate body, medical or academic institution, sports body, social entity or cultural
                                                                                body, and in case of a sports body, cultural body or event, account must be taken of the bona fide of the advertised fixtures or performances and furnish proof.
- An application must be made in writing to the Director-General by the entity for which the applicant will be carrying out work, making the case for why the person cannot make
                                                                                an application for a work permit and furthermore provide reasons why the limited work envisaged is urgent and necessary.
- Section 11(2) authorisation is not a casual work permit, but rather a one-off, non-renewable permission that addresses an immediate short term or urgent need of a limited duration
                                                                                of work activity that cannot be met by an application for a work permit.
- 11(2) will not be granted to any given applicant more than twice in a year.
- Applicants that are exempted for Short Visit / Transit will still need to apply if they wish to obtain an 11(2) visa.
- An application for Section 11(2) will be deemed invalid if the above information is not made evident. A Section 11(2) will be granted at the discretion of the Director-General
                                                                                or delegated authority when considering an application.
- All application forms filled by hand must be original. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.
- The following will not qualify for section 11(2) authorisation; 
- Freelance or self-employed persons
- Contract workers
- Project managers
- Exotic dancers
- Casual labour
- Seasonal labour
- Work-seekers

Overview
ALL APPLICANT MUST APPLY IN PERSON
- Proof of financial – 3 months bank statement, certified by bank and not older than 7 days from the time of application.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete
- There must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- No fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
- For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and
an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths
will be required.
Additional documents
will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable to sign.
- As of the 03 March 2019, the Service Fee has to be paid online when scheduling an appointment to submit the application; without pre-payment of the service fee, you will not be able to secure an appointment. For more information about the pre-payment of the service fees,

- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed that the embassy will grant your request.
- Please note applicants should be aware of the Processing Time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.

- Exchange visa ito Sec 22

- The Mission may request any additional documents / information where and when necessary.
- Consent letter from parent(s) for child/children travelling with one parent or alone needs to be notarized.
- Official passports will be submitted at South African High Commission

## Photo Specifications
 2 photographs must be provided meeting the below specifications
The photo must: 
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35 by 45mm (width x height)
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses: 
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible. 
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress: 
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown. 
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.
                                                                                A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
Normal case processing times
The normal processing time is 10 calendar days.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview

## ALL APPLICANT MUST APPLY IN PERSON
- Proof of financial – 3 months bank statement, certified by bank and not older than 7 days from the time of application.
- Incomplete applications will not be accepted. Please, bear in mind that failing to meet any of the following requirements will also render the application incomplete
- There must be 7 working days before the proposed flight itinerary;
- All supporting documents should be duly certified by the Police in South African Commissioner or Commissioner of Oaths;
- Invitation letter from host in South Africa must be signed;
- No fixed flight arrangements should be made until the visa has been attained.
-  For minors, certified copy of birth certificate, certified copies of both biological parents’ Passports (main page) and an affidavit signed by both biological parents and certified by a Commissioner of Oaths  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3mqaOUVO7YN3WtF8Jlx9Fl/c96dff194e434d07e8dc6fe5f23c1244/Parental-Consent-Affidavit.pdf)will be required. Additional documents (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1qQf4jSjAA1BQeLXcYubEc/2618605633f0283ae6501fb7520e358d/childtravel_1.pdf) will be required in case either of the natural parents is unable to sign. 
- As of the 03 March 2019, the Service Fee has to be paid online when scheduling an appointment to submit the application; without pre-payment of the service fee, you will
                                                                                        not be able to secure an appointment. For more information about the pre-payment of the service fees,
click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/UNKOMwfxlQyaYAaNRZIgl/9fd1c3cd639f2b3e2eab210b137f74cf/FAQ-V-New.pdf)
- Please be aware that all decisions regarding the issuance of, number of entries and duration of a visa are at the sole discretion of the embassy, and it is not guaranteed
                                                                                        that the embassy will grant your request.
- Please note applicants should be aware of the Processing Time for their chosen category of visa and apply accordingly. Please also note that all processing times are minimum
                                                                                        as stated, and in some cases may take longer.
- All application forms filled by hand must be original in BLACK INK only. Scanned copies or signatures of application forms will strictly not be accepted.
